brief_summary: Diptych cross-section comparing the two click mechanisms. Left panel: click-jacket — sleeve riding the stem, catches a ledge inside the housing, plastic tick on press only. Right panel: click-bar — horizontal metal leaf in the lower housing, tripped by a notch on the stem, metallic snap on press and release. Amber accent on the click element in each panel, with sound-strike indicators emanating from the click point.
data_sources: clicky-switches-deep-dive.mdx § 'Click-jacket — the Cherry inheritance' and § 'Click-bar — the Kailh second wind' — 'a small plastic sleeve — the jacket — slides on the stem column inside the housing ... the sleeve striking the housing wall produces the click' / 'a thin metal leaf — the click-bar — is mounted horizontally inside the bottom housing ... the bar snaps against the housing wall'
